businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and commercial AI data analytics platform leader with Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+60%), with US commercial revenue surpassing $650M in Q4 (+137% YoY). The Pentagon designated Maven AI as an official program of record, expanding it across all military branches. FY2026 guidance of $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%) signals accelerating growth, though Forward P/E of 114x remains a key concern.
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) - large enterprise data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - generative AI integration platform
AIP Boot Camps - rapid customer conversion programs

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 11x the S&P 500 average (22x). Extreme premium even vs. SaaS growth peers (40-60x), though 60% revenue growth partially justifies it

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS estimates. Over 2x CrowdStrike (50x) and ServiceNow (55x)

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Guidance of $7.19B-$7.41B. US commercial at +137% is the primary engine. Growth is accelerating

keyMetrics.name: PEG Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: Well above 1.0, indicating overvaluation relative to growth. Premium requires 60%+ growth for years to justify

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: 85x revenue of $4.48B. Pricing reflects defense AI monopoly status

keyMetrics.name: US Commercial Revenue (Q4)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 180 deals over $1M, 61 deals over $10M. AIP boot camps driving rapid conversion

growthDrivers.title: Selected for Golden Dome $185B Missile Defense Software (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Per Reuters, Palantir and Anduril are co-developing software for Trump's $185B Golden Dome antimissile shield. Rosenblatt maintains Buy

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI Designated as Official Program of Record (3/20)
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon officially designated Maven Smart System as a program of record, confirming long-term adoption across all military branches with stable budget allocation

growthDrivers.title: Palantir CTO: 'Iran War is the First AI-Driven Major Conflict' (3/24)
growthDrivers.description: Per Bloomberg, CTO Shyam Sankar called the Iran conflict the first large-scale war where AI played a central role, enabling 2,000+ strikes in 48 hours

growthDrivers.title: UK FCA 3-Month Trial Contract
growthDrivers.description: UK Financial Conduct Authority adopted Palantir Foundry on a 3-month trial, signaling expansion into non-defense government regulatory sectors

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Revenue Guidance $7.19B-$7.41B (+60-65%)
growthDrivers.description: Management expresses confidence in accelerating growth with US commercial as the core engine

growthDrivers.title: US Commercial Revenue Q4 +137% Explosive Growth
growthDrivers.description: Q4 US commercial revenue of $650M (+137% YoY). 180 deals over $1M, 61 over $10M. AIP boot camps are the key conversion driver

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI track record, top-tier security clearances, Maven military adoption, Iran war combat validation. Moats money cannot buy

moats.description: Ontology platform deeply integrated into government/military systems. Replacement costs are prohibitively high once deployed
Top-tier security clearances and 20+ years of government trust. Virtually impossible for new entrants to replicate
Rapid customer acquisition through AIP boot camps and expanding Ontology data model ecosystem
Real-time use in thousands of strikes during the Iran conflict. Combat data is an irreplicable competitive advantage

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup at $60B valuation. Hardware-focused, now collaborating with Palantir on Golden Dome

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competitors, but significantly behind Palantir in government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants entering government AI. Scale advantage but lack Palantir's depth of Ontology platform integration

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Substantially behind Palantir in both revenue and growth rate

recentNews.headline: Palantir Selected for Golden Dome $185B Missile Defense Software
recentNews.significance: Co-developing with Anduril. Software layer for the largest missile defense system in US history. Potential for multi-billion dollar long-term contracts

recentNews.headline: Bloomberg: Palantir CTO Calls Iran War 'First Major AI-Driven Conflict'
recentNews.significance: CTO Shyam Sankar said AI supported 2,000 strikes in 48 hours, marking a paradigm shift in modern warfare

recentNews.headline: Pentagon Designates Maven AI as Official Program of Record
recentNews.significance: Budget allocation and long-term contracting now confirmed. Transition from pilot to permanent military system

recentNews.headline: Rosenblatt Maintains Buy on Golden Dome Contract Potential
recentNews.significance: Golden Dome Phase 1 costs $185B (50% above initial estimates), representing long-term tailwind for Palantir

recentNews.headline: NYT: Silicon Valley Bet on War, and the Bets Are Paying Off
recentNews.significance: Palantir + Anthropic AI technology identified Iran strike targets, reducing targeting time from hours to minutes

analystOpinions.notableComment: Rosenblatt maintains Buy, highlighting Golden Dome potential. However, analyst opinions are extremely polarized between bulls and bears. P/E 252x is the central debate

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: P/E 252x, Forward P/E 114x, PEG 3.19. Difficult to justify even with 60% revenue growth. Over 2x more expensive than CrowdStrike or ServiceNow

risks.title: Defense Dependency Risk
risks.description: Iran war and Golden Dome are increasing defense revenue concentration. War ending could trigger a 'peace discount'

risks.title: Ethical Controversy
risks.description: Growing criticism of AI use in military strikes. ESG fund exclusion risk

risks.title: Insider Selling
risks.description: US Rep. Thomas Suozzi sold PLTR shares. Political risk signal

risks.title: Valuation Collapse if Growth Slows
risks.description: If 60%+ growth decelerates below 50%, PEG deteriorates further, triggering potential sharp valuation correction

buyReasons.title: Golden Dome: $185B Long-Term Contract
buyReasons.rationale: Software layer for the largest missile defense system in US history. Revenue stream that could last decades

buyReasons.title: Maven Official Adoption: Stable Defense Revenue
buyReasons.rationale: Transition from pilot to program of record. Budget allocation confirmed, dramatically reducing contract cancellation risk

buyReasons.title: Iran War Combat Validation
buyReasons.rationale: The only company whose AI supported 2,000 strikes in real combat. Irreplicable competitive moat

buyReasons.title: US Commercial Q4 +137% Explosive Growth
buyReasons.rationale: AIP boot camps accelerating enterprise conversion. Diversifying away from defense dependency

overallOpinion: Selected for the $185B Golden Dome missile defense software, surging 6.8% in a single day. Combined with Maven's official adoption, Palantir's defense AI monopoly is solidifying.
The only company whose AI supported 2,000 strikes in 48 hours during the Iran conflict. The CTO declared it 'the first AI-driven major war,' and the combat validation is undeniable.
Reclaimed the 50-day average ($150) but remains below the 200-day ($169). P/E of 252x means valuation pressure is real. Target $199 (+23%).

investmentVerdict.summary: Golden Dome, Maven, and the Iran war have cemented Palantir's defense AI monopoly. However, at P/E 252x and PEG 3.19, the good news is substantially priced in. Rather than initiating new positions, holding and evaluating after Q1 earnings (May) is the prudent approach.

sources.name: Bloomberg
sources.description: CTO calls Iran war first AI-driven conflict

sources.name: Fox News
sources.description: CTO: AI enabled 2,000 strikes in 48 hours

1/4

2/4

The catch: P/E 252x. Good news may already be priced in. PEG 3.19 says it's expensive relative to growth.

3/4

4/4

witty.text:
$PLTR (3/26) $160.88 (+6.8%)

Golden Dome $185B missile defense. Every time conflict escalates, Palantir goes up. 2,000 AI-assisted strikes in 48 hours, and the CTO calls it 'the first AI war.' Sci-fi is now reality.

But P/E 252x. That's 252 years of earnings baked in. A great company and a great stock aren't always the same.

Target $199 (+23%). May earnings will tell the story.

empathy.hook: PLTR holders, today was a good day
empathy.text:
$PLTR (3/26) $160.88 (+6.8%)

Golden Dome sent it surging. Maven officially adopted. Iran war AI validated. If you've held through the volatility, today was rewarding.

The defense AI moat is undeniable now. But P/E 252x means the rollercoaster continues. Good news rallies followed by earnings pullbacks is a familiar pattern.

Target $199 (+23%). Holders: keep watching. New buyers: dollar-cost averaging is the safe play.
